headline: End Data Center Grid Shifting
full_summary: This bill seeks to address the allocation of grid infrastructure costs, aiming to ensure that large energy users, primarily data centers, bear a more proportionate share of the expenses associated with their substantial energy consumption. It intends to prevent these costs from being disproportionately shifted to residential and smaller commercial ratepayers, potentially leading to increased operating costs for data centers but reducing electricity burdens for other consumers.
supporters: Consumer advocacy groups, residential ratepayers, small businesses, environmental organizations.
opponents: Data center industry, large industrial energy users, economic development agencies.

headline: Report Data Center Water Use
full_summary: This legislation mandates that data centers disclose their water consumption, providing critical transparency regarding their environmental footprint, especially in regions facing water scarcity. The reported data would inform state agencies and policymakers, potentially guiding future water resource management strategies and encouraging more water-efficient practices within the data center industry.
supporters: Environmental groups, water conservationists, local communities, state environmental agencies.
opponents: Some data center operators (due to reporting burden or public scrutiny concerns).

headline: Control Data Center Siting, Noise
full_summary: This bill empowers local governments with greater authority over the placement (siting) of data centers and establishes specific limits on operational noise levels. It aims to mitigate the impact of data center development on nearby residential communities, addressing concerns about quality of life, property values, and environmental noise pollution from facilities such as cooling systems and generators.
supporters: Local residents, community associations, local government officials, quality-of-life advocates.
opponents: Data center developers, commercial real estate investors, some economic development groups.
